verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (Kallikrein-2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It contains a classical signal peptide and is secreted via the conventional secretory pathway. While predominantly found as a soluble secreted protein in the extracellular space (notably in seminal plasma), KLK2 is also expressed on the surface of prostate epithelial and prostate cancer cells, where it has been detected by surface-directed assays. It is a validated target for antibody-based and prostate cancer imaging/therapeutic approaches engaging the cell-surface-associated form. However, since KLK2 is a secreted enzyme without a TM domain or GPI anchor and its surface association is via non-covalent binding to cell-surface components, the strictest reading is secreted_only. But given clinical surface-directed programs and documented cell-surface association in prostate cancer, contextual is the more accurate call.
